manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: honey, draft, scientist, flute, jet, match, glove, washington, nail.
Your opponent's team words are: ring, buck, mercury, post, saturn, tag, face, mole.
Distractor words are: capital, force, opera, tooth, ship, degree, sub.
The kill words are: plastic.

message.content:
CLUE: Air
TARGETS: draft, jet, flute
message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words, not opponent's, distractors, or kill. Must not be any of those words or contain parts of them. Must be one word. Provide list of target words we want to clue (some subset). We can choose any number.

Our words: honey, draft, scientist, flute, jet, match, glove, washington, nail.

We need clue that semantically relates to multiple of these. For example "air" could relate to jet, draft (air draft), scientist? Not exactly. "Metal" maybe relates to nail, jet (metal), match (metal spark). "Paper" could be draft, match? Not.

We could clue "paper" for draft (draft of a paper), scientist (paper), maybe also "match"? No.

"Bee" could relate to honey, but not others.

"Fire" could relate to match, scientist (fire experiments), maybe jet (jet engine flame), but also could relate to opponent "buck"? Not.

We need to avoid clue being part of any listed words. So cannot be "match". Must be distinct.

Maybe we could pick "instrument". Relates to flute, scientist (instrument), maybe also match (instrument?), not great.

Maybe "hand" could relate to glove, nail (fingers), maybe also match (hand match?), but opponent "ring" (hand ring) is an opponent word, dangerous because could cause confusion. Hand clue might lead opponent guess ring (opponent word). That's risky but it's okay if partner guesses wrong? It ends turn and opponent gets a turn, but we want to maximize.

Maybe clue "paper". Relates to draft, scientist (paper), maybe also match (paper match), but guess may be "match" is a noun (matchstick), not paper. "Glove" no.

Maybe clue "flight". Relates to jet, maybe also draft (airflow), maybe also "Washington" (Washington D.C. has Air Force?), not.

Clue "metal". Relates to nail, match (match head contains metal), maybe also scientist (metal research), but also "ring" (opponent) is metal. Could cause opponent guess.

Clue "salmon". Not.

Maybe clue "bee". Relates to honey, scientist (bee research), maybe also glove (bee glove?), no.

Clue "draft". Can't because it's a word in our list.

Clue "steam". Relates to jet (steam turbine), match (fire), scientist (steam experiments). Probably okay.

Clue "paper". I'd choose "paper". Targets: draft, scientist. Also maybe match? Not really. Could add "Washington" (Washington paper money?), but 'paper' could clue Washington (paper as money, Washington on dollar), hmm.

Better maybe "ink". Relates to draft (writing), scientist (ink?), maybe Washington (ink?), not.

What about "research". Relates to scientist, maybe also draft (research paper draft), maybe also match? Not.

Goal is to give one clue. Could pick "flight". Targets: jet, draft (air draft). Might also relate to "glove" (flight glove?), no.

We could give multiple separate clues in separate turns but here only one. We'll just give clue "air". But "air" is not any listed word. Targets: draft (air draft), jet (aircraft), flute (air passes through), maybe scientist (air research), honey? No. Might be okay.

However "air" could associate with opponent "ring"? No.

But "air" could be ambiguous. Maybe choose "instrument". Targets: flute, scientist (instrument), maybe glove (instrument?), not.

I think best is "air". So:

CLUE: Air
TARGETS: draft, jet, flute

Check that "air" is not part of any list words: no. Good.

Proceed.

Provide a comma-separated list of up to 3 words from the following list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'air'. Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text in your answer.

mole, nail, post, opera, saturn, sub, draft, tag, washington, match, plastic, degree, flute, face, force, honey, scientist, mercury, glove, jet, buck, capital, ring, tooth, ship
